question: Install Git on Ubuntu using apt-get.
hint: You may need to update the package list first.

question: Set your global Git user.name to 'Alice Example'.
validation_script:
#!/binbash
NAME=$(git config --global --get user.name 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$NAME" = "Alice Example" ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi

question: Set your global Git user.email to 'alice@example.com'.
validation_script:
#!/binbash
EMAIL=$(git config --global --get user.email 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$EMAIL" = "alice@example.com" ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi

question: Configure Git to use 'main' as the default branch for new repositories.
validation_script:
#!/binbash
BRANCH=$(git config --global --get init.defaultBranch 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$BRANCH" = "main" ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi

question: Verify your Git configuration by listing all settings and confirming user.name, user.email, and init.defaultBranch are correct.
validation_script:
#!/binbash
OUTPUT=$(git config --list 2>/dev/null)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "^user.name=Alice Example$" && \
   echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "^user.email=alice@example.com$" && \
   echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "^init.defaultBranch=main$"; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
